arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line debuginfod-find 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; dpkg -l | grep -E 'binutils|llvm' 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try Microsoft's symbol server for the debug symbols
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
LIB="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try ELF build-id format on Microsoft symbol server
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${LIB}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${LIB}" 2>&1
echo ""

# Try sym variant  
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${LIB}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${LIB}" 2>&1
echo ""

# Try debuginfod
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
  "https://debuginfod.elfutils.org/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo" 2>&1
echo ""
arguments.description: Try fetching debug symbols from symbol servers

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try fetching debug symbols from symbol servers

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The key library is `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. The frames are at offsets:

- #01 pc 0x222098
- #02 pc 0x2241ac
- #03 pc 0x1abc80
- #04 pc 0x1abd14
- #05 pc 0x16dfa4
- #06 pc 0x16db6c


- #07 pc 0x16de04

To resolve these, I need the debug symbols for this specific build. Microsoft hosts symbol files for .NET runtime binaries on their symbol server, which I can query using the BuildId and filename to retrieve the corresponding debug information.

I could try constructing the URL for Microsoft's symbol server or use the debuginfod protocol to fetch the symbols directly, though without knowing the exact .NET version, this becomes more challenging. Let me explore what's available through the NuGet packages or symbol servers. I'm realizing this tombstone has a fake BuildId, so finding actual debug symbols will be difficult. Let me try a few approaches—checking for available tools like `addr2line` or `llvm-symbolizer`, attempting to fetch symbols from Microsoft's server, and if that fails, I'll explain what the user would need to do to properly symbolicate these frames.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line debuginfod-find 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; dpkg -l | grep -E 'binutils|llvm' 2>/dev/null | head -5

# Try Microsoft's symbol server for the debug symbols
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
LIB="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try ELF build-id format on Microsoft symbol server
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${LIB}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${LIB}" 2>&1
echo ""

# Try sym variant  
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${LIB}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${LIB}" 2>&1
echo ""

# Try debuginfod
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
  "https://debuginfod.elfutils.org/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo" 2>&1
echo ""
arguments.description: Try fetching debug symbols from symbol servers

# Try Microsoft's symbol server for the debug symbols
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
LIB="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try ELF build-id format on Microsoft symbol server
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${LIB}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${LIB}" 2>&1
echo ""

# Try sym variant  
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${LIB}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${LIB}" 2>&1
echo ""

# Try debuginfod
curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
  "https://debuginfod.elfutils.org/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo" 2>&1
echo ""

arguments.command:
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
WORKDIR="/tmp/sv-4949866140c947a68316b6d3254c1f69/symbols"

# Try various debug info file names on the symbol server
for NAME in "_.debug" "libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" "libmonosgen-2.0.so.debug" "libmonosgen-2.0.so.dwarf"; do
  CODE=$(curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${NAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${NAME}" 2>/dev/null)
  echo "$NAME: $CODE"
done

# Also try the .so.dbg with sym variant
for NAME in "_.debug" "libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" "libmonosgen-2.0.so.debug"; do
  CODE=$(curl -sS -f -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${NAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${NAME}" 2>/dev/null)
  echo "$NAME (sym): $CODE"
done

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Check what symbols exist in the stripped binary (dynamic symbols)
WORKDIR="/tmp/sv-4949866140c947a68316b6d3254c1f69/symbols"
# We need an aarch64 nm/readelf; but readelf should work cross-arch
readelf -sW "$WORKDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>/dev/null | head -5
echo "---"
readelf -sW "$WORKDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>/dev/null | wc -l
echo "---"
# Check if dynsym has the functions we need
readelf --dyn-syms -W "$WORKDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>/dev/null | wc -l
echo "---"
# Try to find symbols near our addresses
for ADDR in 0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04; do
  echo -n "$ADDR: "
  readelf --dyn-syms -W "$WORKDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>/dev/null | \
    awk -v addr="$ADDR" '
    BEGIN { addr_dec = strtonum(addr) }
    $4 == "FUNC" { 
      sym_addr = strtonum("0x"$2); 
      sym_size = strtonum($3);
      if (addr_dec >= sym_addr && addr_dec < sym_addr + sym_size) {
        printf "%s+0x%x (%s)\n", $8, addr_dec - sym_addr, $8
      }
    }'
done
arguments.description: Check dynamic symbols for function names

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Check dynamic symbols for function names

arguments.command:
WORKDIR="/tmp/sv-4949866140c947a68316b6d3254c1f69/symbols"

# Symbolicate all mono frames using the debug symbol file
for ADDR in 0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04; do
  echo -n "$ADDR: "
  addr2line -f -e "$WORKDIR/_.debug" -C "$ADDR"
  echo "---"
done

data.reasoningText:
We got the debug symbols with `debug_info` and `not stripped`. Now let me use `addr2line` to symbolicate the addresses. But since this is an ARM aarch64 binary and we're on x86_64, I need to check if we have an aarch64 addr2line or if the regular one handles it.

Actually, `addr2line` should work cross-architecture since it just reads DWARF debug info from ELF files. Let me try.

data.content:
Here's the symbolicated backtrace for the `libmonosgen-2.0.so` frames:

```
#00 pc 0x6ae1c   libc.so                (abort+164)
#01 pc 0x222098  libmonosgen-2.0.so     ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast        (icall.c:6244)
#02 pc 0x2241ac  libmonosgen-2.0.so     ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast_raw    (icall-def.h:202)
#03 pc 0x1abc80  libmonosgen-2.0.so     do_icall                                     (interp.c:2457)
#04 pc 0x1abd14  libmonosgen-2.0.so     get_interp_to_native_trampoline              (interp.c:1732)
#05 pc 0x16dfa4  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                (type-checking.c)
#06 pc 0x16db6c  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                (type-checking.c:506)
#07 pc 0x16de04  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                (type-checking.c:528)
#08 pc 0x307c    <anonymous/JIT>        (managed code)
```

**Summary:** The crash is a `SIGABRT` triggered by `Environment.FailFast` ("not combatible"). The call chain shows the Mono interpreter executing a native icall (`do_icall`) for `FailFast`, preceded by `handle_isinst` type-checking frames — suggesting a type compatibility check failed on startup, causing the app to abort.
